commands.review_summary:
The PR extends Google thinking payload sanitization from `google-generative-ai` to `google-vertex` and `google-gemini-cli`, with focused SDK stream-wrapper coverage and non-Google passthrough coverage.

Reproducibility: yes. from source, not from a live run: current main shares Google thinking hooks across the provider aliases but only sanitizes `google-generative-ai` payload APIs, leaving the reported Vertex/Gemini CLI API shapes unsanitized.
commands.review_followup: No automated code repair is indicated; maintainers should require/perform real provider proof or explicitly override that gate before merging this protected-label PR.

target.title: fix: sanitize Google thinking payloads for Vertex
target.body:
## Summary

- Extends the Google thinking payload sanitizer to `google-vertex` and `google-gemini-cli` APIs in addition to `google-generative-ai`.
- Preserves passthrough behavior for non-Google APIs.
- Adds focused SDK stream-wrapper coverage for the new API identifiers.

Closes #38327.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/google-stream-wrappers.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/extra-params.google.test.ts extensions/google/provider-models.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-stream.test.ts`
- `codex review --uncommitted`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Google Vertex and Gemini CLI streaming payloads now receive the same thinking-config sanitization as Google Generative AI payloads.

Real environment tested: WSL-native checkout at `origin/main` `b86435f0b5`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran format, `git diff --check`, focused Google/plugin-sdk Vitest, then `codex review --uncommitted` in `/root/openclaw-wsl-google-38327`.

Evidence after fix: Focused Vitest passed 5 files / 47 tests; Codex review reported no actionable regression.

Observed result after fix: `google-generative-ai`, `google-vertex`, and `google-gemini-cli` payloads convert invalid thinking budget payloads to the expected thinking level; non-Google APIs keep the original payload unchanged.

What was not tested: Full CI was not run locally; this was scoped to the affected Google stream-wrapper and related Google model tests.
